title: Who was your favorite of the three ADV Dub Tojis
display_title: Who was your favorite of the three ADV Dub Tojis
wikitext:
[]: Who was your favorite of the three ADV Dub Tojis?             Quote Post by Gus Hanson » Tue Dec 24, 2013 2:13 pm With me, the undisputed king is Joe Pisano. He pulls off Toji's jockness with such flair that it's hard for me to find anyone who can top it. Michael O'Connor's Toji sounds like it's trying way too hard to copy Joe in every sense of the word. As for Brett Weaver, he just sounds like his generic Brett Weaver self when voicing Toji that it sounds like every other role he's played. Not much variety to it which isn't fun. How about the rest of you?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Dec 24, 2013 2:16 pm Original Toji is Tomokazu Seki

[]:             Quote Post by Gus Hanson » Tue Dec 24, 2013 2:29 pm I'm talking about the three original ADV English dub Tojis. I really should've clarified it in my title, sorry about that.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Tue Dec 24, 2013 3:48 pm What is a dub?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Dec 24, 2013 4:04 pm Guy Nacks wrote: What is a dub?

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Tue Dec 24, 2013 4:58 pm Same here. I...guess being Toji isn't that difficult?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Dec 24, 2013 5:45 pm There's more than one?

[]:             Quote Post by Gus Hanson » Tue Dec 24, 2013 6:30 pm *sighs* Look, if this is not gonna be taken seriously then lock it for all i care.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Dec 24, 2013 6:36 pm I'm serious. I had no idea there was more than one voice actor playing him. It's not like were talking Hyuga and Jive Turkey Hyuga here.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Dec 24, 2013 7:02 pm I only knew there were multiple English VAs for Toji because of the audio commentary.

[]:             Quote Post by Gus Hanson » Tue Dec 24, 2013 8:41 pm I apologize if i came off as rude. Yeah, it's true. Joe Pisano played Toji from episodes 3-18, Michael O'Connor did it from episodes 19 to 20, and Brett Weaver voiced him in the last two episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by Ringworm128 » Wed Dec 25, 2013 7:45 am Justin Cook is better than them all. Though it would be neat to here someone with a southern accent or something to match his Kansai accent.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Dec 25, 2013 3:57 pm Chuckman wrote: There's more than one?

[]:             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Thu Dec 26, 2013 1:13 am Gus Hanson wrote: Joe Pisano played Toji from episodes 3-18, Michael O'Connor did it from episodes 19 to 20, and Brett Weaver voiced him in the last two episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Dec 26, 2013 2:19 am Episodes 03 and 04 are the only NGE I actually had to watch dubbed to start with -- the guy in town we used to rent anime from ordered the wrong tape -- so I'll go with Mr. Pisano.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Dec 26, 2013 9:42 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Original Toji is Tomokazu Seki

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Fri Dec 27, 2013 4:51 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I only knew there were multiple English VAs for Toji because of the audio commentary.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Fri Dec 27, 2013 10:29 pm I felt that the performances of all three ADV voice actors were actually pretty good, considering the few lines of dialogue they each had to work with. In particular, the "Don't you have any balls at all?!!" line from Episode 4 always manages to elicit a chuckle out of me. IMO Justin Cook's Funimation-approved voice sounded wrong coming out of that character's mouth. It just wasn't deep enough for what I expected that character to sound like. Hey, sue me. However, I must admit to a certain affectionate bias toward Brett Weaver, if for no other reason than his hilarious portrayal of Gai Daigoji from Martian Successor Nadesico, as well as Nabeshin from Excel Saga.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sat Dec 28, 2013 4:44 am What I can't get over is that they had Brett Weaver voice extras for Rebuild but they couldn't be assed to have him do Toji again.
